programmes.show_description: NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
More than a decade after the Great East Japan Earthquake, two women who lost their daughters ask a painter to portray them as grown-ups - will the portraits bring solace to the bereaved families?
Chef Nobuko, her cameraman husband Makoto, and their cat Tama take us on a tasty journey of local cuisine served with a side of seasonal scenery at the Satoyama Kitchen guesthouse in Hida Takayama.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
English-speaking visitors travel the length of Japan exploring the local culture, meeting the people, and offering travel hints rarely found in guidebooks.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Learn how to make mouth-watering katsu beef fried with tempura flour and panko breadcrumbs using Chef Rika's recipe, then try matcha ice cream afterwards!
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
An amusement arcade near Tokyo has row after row of token-operated games - for three days we ask people about the appeal of games that offer no cash or prizes no matter how many tokens are won.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Japanese researchers have uncovered the mechanism behind vitamin D's bone-strengthening effects, and its potential impact on cancer is also becoming apparent - we explore the power of vitamin D.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Learn how to make the most of your Japanese skills - watch our drama that features useful communication strategies, our snappy videos about onomatopoeia, and our documentaries on 'senpai' in Japan.
With cherry blossoms in full bloom, there are many tastes only to be found in Spring. Go for a stroll in the soft light and gentle breezes in this beautiful Japanese season.
Featured recipe: White Mizu-yokan with Matcha Syrup. Learn about Japanese home cooking with Master Chef Saito, based on traditional Japanese cooking techniques in five minutes!
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
English-speaking visitors travel the length of Japan exploring the local culture, meeting the people, and offering travel hints rarely found in guidebooks.
This daily news programme picks up the key news stories of the past 24 hours from Japan and around Asia.

programmes.show_name: Bosai: Science That Can Save Your Life
programmes.show_description: Lava Flows: The hazard map for Mount Fuji was revised in 2021, revealing the chance of more damage from lava flows - we look at what lies behind the assumptions for this assessment.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Japanese researchers have uncovered the mechanism behind vitamin D's bone-strengthening effects, and its potential impact on cancer is also becoming apparent - we explore the power of vitamin D.
An evening news bulletin that provides Japan's views on many domestic and international news events.
Our special Darwin series continues following 3 male lions on separate paths toward adulthood and their destinies. In Part 4, we track their search for allies necessary in any quest for the throne.
Hirosaki Park in Aomori Prefecture has been a beloved spot to see sakura blossoms for over 100 years - see the multifaceted beauty of sakura blossoms through time-lapse photography.
This programme gives you comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia, explaining the day's developments and what is behind them.
Filmmaker Soda Kazuhiro, whose latest work won the Best Movie award at a film festival in 2024, tells us about the 'observational' documentary style he has been championing for 20 years.
The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture - this episode looks at words related to Japanese sweets. Explore the unique culture behind them.
#14 I want to go to Japan someday. / A verb with 'te mitai desu' means 'want to do something that you haven't done before'. 'Nihon e itte mitai desu' means 'I want to go to Japan someday'.

In this episode, we learn about the spring dates of seimei and kokuu. The temple chefs show us how to make two seasonal specialties, yomogi rice cakes and bamboo shoots mixed with sansho leaves.
Discover the story behind the 36 Views of Mount Fuji woodprint series. The Great Wave off Kanagawa by Hokusai is not just a huge wave, and the boat caught in the wave is there for a reason.
The number of elderly foreigners who grapple with language difficulties and isolation is rising in Shinjuku, Tokyo. We take a look at the new forms of social support driven by the local community.

Hino in western Tokyo is known for irrigation canals and its past as a rice cultivating centre during the Edo period - the fertile lands gave rise to the men who would form the legendary Shinsengumi.

Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter - this time, smokeless grills.

Social entrepreneur Ishikawa Naohito creates opportunities for Sri Lankans to enrich their lives - projects include a massage salon employing the visually impaired and an organic vegetable business.

Sake is made by fermenting steamed rice - we visit a 350-year-old brewery to gain an insight into the reverence that the brewers have toward nature and microbes.
We visit Lake Biwa, Japan's largest and oldest lake, as a cat leads us to an overlook with a great view of the lake and another cat hides among the multitude of wares at a Shigaraki ceramics shop.

programmes.show_name: Bosai: Science That Can Save Your Life
programmes.show_description: Lava Flows: The hazard map for Mount Fuji was revised in 2021, revealing the chance of more damage from lava flows - we look at what lies behind the assumptions for this assessment.
Wild elephants entering human living areas, causing accidents and crop damage, is a problem across Asia. What will happen to elephant-human coexistence as their habitats shift due to climate change?
Hagi ware has been cherished in tea ceremonies for over 400 years. Cracks in the glaze create patterns whose colour evolves with time and use. Watch masterful pottery production in time-lapse.
This programme gives you comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia, explaining the day's developments and what is behind them.
Nagasaka Mago makes art with discarded smartphones, computers, and other e-waste from the slums of Ghana, then uses much of the proceeds to give back to the community - he talks about his vision.
Introducing asari, Japan's most popular clam - visit Aichi to feast your eyes on asari dishes and see how they're cultivated using suspended cages. How about diving for some giant asari?
Discover the story behind the 36 Views of Mount Fuji woodprint series. The Great Wave off Kanagawa by Hokusai is not just a huge wave, and the boat caught in the wave is there for a reason.

Eggplants (nasu) take the spotlight - enjoy juicy mizu-nasu and melt-in-your-mouth Kamo nasu, and discover the surprising range of Japanese eggplants from traditional shojin ryori to sweets.
The top three geisha idol celebrities in Edo are Tomimoto Toyohina, Takashima Ohisa, and Naniwaya Kita; see how Kitagawa Utamaro, the master of female beauty, has illustrated them so differently.
